commits.message:
mit-styling-aj1.7.11: the em — a unit grid fills to a sort's measured set width

Recast the waffle node (generic amber 10x10 grid filling easeOut(p)*100 cells
to a meaningless '47% finish a good one') into THE EM: the em square drawn as a
10x10 unit grid (100 units) that fills in reading order to the MEASURED set
width of a specimen serif sort — its advance as a share of the em, taken at
build via measureText from the on-screen Georgia, never an asserted figure. Lit
cells are the glyph's body; outlined dark cells are the side-bearing air that
spaces type. Brought into the jade specimen vocabulary (accent jade, crop-mark
frame, tracked-mono catalogue label, caption riding inside) like its siblings
annotate/chart.

Distinct mechanic: a unit grid that FILLS to a part-of-whole — not chart's bars
that GROW on an axis, no axis/gridline/value-chip furniture reused. ONE scalar
(scaled = clamp(n.w/0.9,0,1)*units) drives the lit-cell count, the live
'SET k/100 UNITS' readout, and when the caption turns from identity to lesson,
so picture/number/words can never disagree. Fills on n.w (read-beat) so the
empty em is read first; fill transition auto-gated by the global reduce-motion
kill, no new WAAPI. Also dropped the now-stale waffle '47%' example from the
read-beat SCOPE comment (waffle now tracks n.w, not raw p).

Verified in Chrome at 810px across the node's scroll range [78370, 80699]:
SET tracks 0 -> 25 -> 50 in exact lockstep with the lit cells; 'a' measures to
a clean 50/100; zero console errors on fresh load and a full-document sweep.

mit-styling-aj1.7.10: the frequency table — a chart of the printer's case redraws itself (#33)

* mit-styling-aj1.7.10: the frequency table — a chart of the printer's case redraws itself

Recast the basic 3-bar chart (invented values, rainbow gradients, no frame) into
THE FREQUENCY TABLE: English letter frequency — E T A O I N · S H R D L U, the
order of the printer's type case — redrawing one sort at a time. The empty ruled
table (value axis, dashed gridlines, per-cent ticks) is read first through the
read-beat; then each sort grows as a flat matte-jade column topped by its glyph
set in the specimen serif, the currently-set sort lifting to bright jade.

- ONE scalar (scaled = clamp(n.w/0.9)*N) drives bar height, the live SET count,
  the highlighted sort AND the caption [LAW:one-source-of-truth] — table, number
  and words can never disagree.
- One SVG (viewBox 200x100) so every bar provably stands on the shared axis;
  geometry derives from one baseline + value scale, no hand-placed y's.
- Wears the shared jade specimen vocabulary (8-strip crop-mark frame at positive
  inset, tracked-mono catalogue label); accent jade, tempo movement (12 sorts
  earn the longest breath).
- Distinct mechanic: a QUANTITATIVE graphic whose bars GROW — not annotate's
  static callouts, waffle's unit grid, count's ticking figures, draw's stroke.
- Pure scroll-driven; the live-bar fill transition is auto-gated by the global
  prefers-reduced-motion kill, no extra REDUCE_MOTION branch.

Verified in Chrome at 810px across the full scroll range: zero console errors.

* chart: derive the resting caption's sort sequence from node.sorts

The rest line embedded 'E T A O I N · S H R D L U' as a literal — a second
copy of the glyph sequence already authoritative in node.sorts (each .g), free
to drift from it. Derive the sequence in build() from sorts.map(s=>s.g), split
at the midpoint (the ETAOIN / SHRDLU break falls out of N/2), computed once from
the data. The literal sequence is gone; one source of truth for the sorts.

[LAW:one-source-of-truth]
